settings.strapline: A youth-focused community arts centre in Boscombe, Bournemouth
settings.announcement: Summer Holiday Arts Club bookings are live! HAF funded places available — book early.
settings.address: Honeycombe Arts Hub, Units 4 & 5, The Sovereign Shopping Centre, 600 Christchurch Road, Boscombe, Bournemouth BH1 4SX

events.title: Summer Holiday Arts Club
events.summary: A fun-filled summer of combined arts & crafts, trips and visiting artists. Full days 8am–5pm, HAF funded places 10am–3pm.
events.description:
Kid's Club is great fun — members make new friends and build confidence as budding artists. Holiday Arts Club opens weekdays from 8am–5pm. You must be a member to join: £15 per child per year, with free membership for families eligible for Free School Meals.

We are open Monday 27th July – Friday 28th August 2026. Full-day spaces run 8am–5pm and include breakfast (please pack a lunch). HAF funded hours are 10am–3pm and include breakfast, a hot lunch and two arts activities a day.

We have been awarded BCP Council HAF funding for any parent in receipt of Free School Meals. To apply, TEXT your child's full name, date of birth and postcode with the heading 'HAF Apply' to 07932 772905.

Weekly planners highlighting daily activities are released three weeks prior on our social media and by direct email to families. If your child has any SEMH, SEND or additional needs, please give us a call so we can chat about how best to support them.

We are listed on the Voluntary Ofsted Register EY496668 — parents receiving Working Tax Credits or Universal Credit can usually claim back up to 75% of childcare costs via HMRC. Paying with childcare vouchers? Please apply at least two weeks before club starts.

events.title: Home Education Programme
events.summary: Creative learning days for home-educated children — fine arts, communication, expression, theatre and media skills.
events.description:
Join us every Thursday, 9am–12pm at the Hub in Boscombe for exciting creative sessions per age group, designed for young people who are home educated.

Our programme balances arts & crafts sessions with confidence-building communication and group-skills sessions in a fun, supportive environment. Morning groups welcome ages 5–10, with two sessions and a break.

The next term runs 4th June – 23rd July 2026. Booking is essential for attendance.

Why join?
• Supportive, small group environment
• Build confidence, creativity & communication
• Develop practical skills in arts, crafts & media

£5 per session for all attendees. Please email Poppy at info@honeycombeartshub.org.uk for more info and help with bookings — we will purchase a yearly membership for you, and then you'll have access to booking our home ed sessions. For student and financial support contact info@honeycombeartshub.org.uk.

events.title: Cosplay Sewing Course
events.summary: Learn the art of sewing in relaxed weekly workshops — fabrics, materials and machine skills for budding cosplay builders.
events.description:
Get creative and learn the art of sewing in our relaxed weekly workshops for ages 12+. Explore fabrics, materials and sewing-machine skills while building confidence and developing your own creative style.

Whether you're a complete beginner or looking to grow your skills, these hands-on sessions are the perfect place to learn, make and create. Bring your own fabrics, and feel free to bring your own sewing machine — some machines are also available to use.

Every Thursday, 5:00pm–6:30pm. £10 per session (cash). Please RSVP via Facebook @Hand-Made.co.

events.title: Creative Youth Committee — Project 14–22
events.summary: Portfolio building, paid arts work, mentoring and a real say in how the Hub is run — for young creatives aged 14–22.
events.description:
The 14–22 Project gives young people chances to develop their portfolio, creative skills, work opportunities and community engagement — as well as communication and group skills with like-minded young people in your community.

Volunteer: enrich our growing community — volunteer at arts events and exhibitions, gain experience working with children and young people, try new things and gain new skills.

Paid work: support special events — gain freelance arts work at events, work with young people at our Holiday Arts Club, sell your artwork to the community, programme and lead workshops and community events.

Support: help with artistic portfolios and professional development, free use of our space and equipment for your own projects, university references and monthly group meetings.

Get in touch today for more information for you or your teenager to get involved in the next meeting.

events.title: Drawing & Talking Therapy
events.summary: Gentle 1:1 and small-group creative wellbeing support, helping children process feelings through art.
events.description:
Our expanded Drawing & Talking programme offers 1:1 and small-group creative wellbeing support in a safe, calm space at the Hub.

Drawing & Talking is a gentle, child-led therapeutic approach: children draw with a trusted practitioner and talk about their drawing if they wish, helping them process feelings that are hard to put into words.

Sessions run weekly in blocks of 12. Places are limited and allocated based on need — fully funded places are available for families who need them most.

To make a referral or find out more, contact info@honeycombeartshub.org.uk or speak to a member of the team.

events.title: Saturday Art Club
events.summary: Drop in on Saturdays for open-studio making — painting, craft, clay, collage and more with our friendly team.
events.description:
Our Hub is open Saturdays 9am–7pm for open-studio creative sessions. Drop in and explore painting, craft, clay, collage, printing and more, supported by our friendly team of artists and volunteers.

All materials are provided. Annual membership (£15 per child) gives families access to all our arts programmes and events throughout the year.

Check availability before you travel — Saturdays can get busy! Call, text or email the team to confirm.

events.title: Kids' Cinema & Parties
events.summary: Birthday parties in our studio cinema — huge screen, beanbags, popcorn and party bags for up to 30 kids.
events.description:
We host cinema parties and screenings in our state-of-the-art studios in Boscombe. The 1,350 sq ft space boasts a huge 4 × 3.5 metre cinema screen and a laser projector with high-definition 1080p resolution — complemented by a hair-raising soundbar and trouser-flapping bass. Children kick back and relax in uber-comfortable beanbag chairs.

The birthday girl or boy chooses a film from our extensive catalogue. We cater for children aged 6–15, and you don't need to be a member to book. All party guests receive a special party bag including a drink, popcorn, sweets, notepad, stickers and crayons.

Parents leave their children in our capable hands for the duration of the film, freeing up some special time for mums and dads to have a break. The venue accommodates up to 30 kids per party — all this for £250! Booking enquiries by phone or at reception. We can adapt the booking to your needs — ask us about add-ons.

events.title: Youth Advisory Board
events.summary: A new group of young creatives shaping the Hub's direction — youth voice at the heart of everything we do.
events.description:
Our Youth Advisory Board is a new group of young creatives who help shape our direction, influence programming and make sure youth voice is at the heart of everything we do at Honeycombe Arts Hub.

Board members meet monthly, work alongside our trustees and team, and lead on projects that matter to young people — from programming new workshops to shaping our Media Centre.

Interested in joining? We'd love to hear from you. Email info@honeycombeartshub.org.uk or speak to any member of the team.

events.title: Summer Exhibition 2026
events.summary: Our end-of-summer celebration — young artists exhibit their work to family, friends and the community.
events.description:
Every holiday ends with a joyful celebration of creativity, and this summer we're going big: a public exhibition of work made by young people across our Summer Holiday Arts Club, courses and clubs.

Expect painting, sculpture, textiles, film, photography and live performance — all made by young artists aged 5 to 22. Family, friends and the whole community are warmly invited.

Free entry. Refreshments available. Keep an eye on our social media for the full programme.

pastEvents.title: Under the Sea — Drama Showcase
pastEvents.description: Our drama group dived deep for a mermaid-themed showcase, with costumes, sets and songs all made by the cast at the Hub.

pastEvents.title: Easter Holiday Arts Club
pastEvents.description: Two weeks of spring crafts, clay crowns, flower wreaths and a grand Easter bonnet parade through the Sovereign Centre.

pastEvents.title: Christmas Holiday Club
pastEvents.description: Festive painting, ornament making and our annual Christmas party — including hand-painted decorations for the community tree.

pastEvents.title: Halloween Costume Spectacular
pastEvents.description: A day of dressing up, plate spinning, circus skills and spooky crafts — with prizes for the most creative homemade costume.

pastEvents.title: Summer Holiday Arts Club 2025
pastEvents.description: Six weeks of art, music, drama, trips and new friendships — ending in a showcase where every young artist presented their portfolio.

pastEvents.title: Ukulele Course & Concert
pastEvents.description: Families learned ukulele together over six weeks, finishing with a concert and certificates for every performer.

pastEvents.title: Members' Pizza Awards Day
pastEvents.description: We celebrated our members' achievements with a pizza-making day out — certificates, weaving crafts and a lot of cheese.

pastEvents.title: Multi-Cultural Arts Summer
pastEvents.description: A summer celebrating arts from around the world, with visiting practitioners leading dance, drumming, textiles and food art.

pastEvents.title: Flying Seagulls Project
pastEvents.description: The legendary Flying Seagulls brought circus, clowning and chaos — plate spinning, wigs and all — to Boscombe.

pastEvents.title: Cabinet of Living Cinema
pastEvents.description: Live music meets film: our young people scored and performed a soundtrack to silent cinema with the Cabinet of Living Cinema.

pastEvents.title: Beside the Seaside!
pastEvents.description: Willow weaving, beach art and outdoor making — a socially-distanced summer of fresh-air creativity.

pastEvents.title: Digital Arts Workshops
pastEvents.description: Film, animation and digital design workshops in partnership with local studios — the seed of our new Media Centre.

testimonials.quote: This is the first time the kids have been anywhere like this... and the point is, there is nowhere else like this! They had such a wonderful time they are going twice next week too. Great staff, great activities, GREAT IDEA! 5 stars!
testimonials.author: Kirsty Sharples

testimonials.quote: A fantastic charity doing wonderful work within our community. We have seen first-hand the commitment and dedication of both staff and volunteers. They make a difference in our very deprived community.
testimonials.author: Patsy Hallmey

testimonials.quote: Your service and set up for the children has literally been a lifeline for me. It has given me structure and respite — I feel very grateful and thankful for how you have helped me and my daughter.
testimonials.author: Parent

testimonials.quote: The activities are so imaginative and diverse, and it's great that real-life creative practitioners come in to work with the children. We've been blown away by what our daughter has produced — her sense of pride, achievement and growing confidence.
testimonials.author: Catherine Clarke

testimonials.quote: It's fun because you can use your imagination!
testimonials.author: Blake

testimonials.quote: I love being a staff member here. It's an awesome place to work and I always have a brilliant time making and creating art with the kids.
testimonials.author: Emma

testimonials.quote: My daughter has MLD and it is fantastic that she has been so well included in the activities.
testimonials.author: Member's parent

testimonials.quote: I really had a good time and I made a best friend. I had a really good summer.
testimonials.author: Abie

testimonials.quote: Amazing staff, amazing opportunity for children to get creative and learn. Can't wait to book in for more activities.
testimonials.author: Lydia Harris

testimonials.quote: The kids were inspirational — it fuelled creativity and confidence.
testimonials.author: Caine

testimonials.quote: I love it because there is lots to do, something new every day. You make loads of friends. If you are bored at home, come here — there's always something to do.
testimonials.author: Ellie

testimonials.quote: Both of my daughters loved the varied activities and going out. Great effort from all the staff, and the early start really helps.
testimonials.author: Anita

impact.label: fully funded arts education places & 1:1 therapy sessions
impact.detail: Offered to local children in need at our Hub in Boscombe and at national festivals in 2024 — giving respite to parents struggling financially, emotionally or physically.

impact.label: subscribed members accessed the Hub
impact.detail: 84% of whom identify as living in poverty — accessing high-quality arts activities and working towards national Arts Award qualifications.

impact.label: national arts qualifications achieved in 2024
impact.detail: 60 Discover, 30 Explore, 7 Bronze and 1 Silver Arts Awards — evidencing creativity, critical thinking, collaboration and leadership. Moderated by Trinity College London.

impact.label: volunteers supporting live projects
impact.detail: College, university and international students, parents, grandparents, business professionals and our own alumni returning as Ambassadors of the Arts.

values.title: Radical Inclusion
values.text: Open, welcoming spaces for every child — prioritising those with particular need by reason of age, disability or social and economic circumstances.

values.title: Imagination First
values.text: Spaces that spark imagination and build confidence, blending traditional arts with new media and emerging technologies.

values.title: Youth Voice
values.text: Young people shape what we do through voice, choice and collaboration — from our Youth Advisory Board to Project 14–22.

values.title: Wellbeing & Belonging
values.text: Safe spaces where young people thrive socially and artistically, with a focus on self-expression, resilience and community.
